_meta.description: Project type profiles with dimension weight overrides, skipped/critical items, and template recommendations. Profiles are grouped by common audit characteristics — variants preserve fine-grained distinctions for specialized use.
_meta.usage: Load the matching profile to adjust audit weights and skip irrelevant items. All 8 weights are explicit per profile and sum to 1.0. Use variants only when the project clearly fits a sub-category.
_meta.calibration_notes: Weights reflect the relative importance of each dimension for the project type, derived from: (1) industry incident analysis (supply chain attacks, production outages, data breaches), (2) control theory leverage (mechanical constraints have highest leverage on agent code quality), (3) diminishing returns (observability adds little value for non-service code). Initial weights were calibrated against 8 real-repo audits documented in evals/evals.json: OpenClaw x4 (v2.0.0-beta5, v2026.1.30, v2026.2.26, v2026.3.31), OpenCode (v1.3.13), OpenFang (v0.5.7), Cherry Studio (v1.8.4), Paperclip (v2026.325.0). Weights are adjusted as more audit data accumulates. Each profile includes a weight_rationale field that documents the specific reasoning behind its weight distribution — including which dimensions were elevated or reduced and why. When a weight changes between versions, the rationale field is updated to reflect the change and its motivation (e.g., library dim8_safety was elevated from 0.02 to 0.05 based on supply chain incident data).

frontend.name: Frontend Application
frontend.description: SPA, SSR/SSG, or browser extension (React, Vue, Svelte, Angular, Next.js, Nuxt, Astro)
frontend.weight_rationale: UI visibility and E2E testing are elevated because frontend agents frequently break visual layouts and user flows that only browser-level checks can catch. Observability slightly reduced since frontend services generate fewer server-side signals.

substitute_items.3.2: Client-side error tracking and performance monitoring (Sentry, LogRocket, etc.)

frontend.notes: UI visibility (3.4) and E2E testing (4.5) are high priority. Dependency direction applies to component architecture.

spa.name: Single-Page Application
spa.description: Pure SPA (React, Vue, Svelte, Angular) — no server-side rendering
spa.notes: Server-side metrics (3.2) less relevant; substitute with client-side error tracking.

ssr.name: SSR/SSG Framework
ssr.description: Server-side rendered or static site (Next.js, Nuxt, SvelteKit, Astro)
ssr.notes: Hybrid rendering means both server observability and client E2E matter.

browser-extension.name: Browser Extension
browser-extension.notes: Security is elevated due to browser API access.

backend.name: Backend Service
backend.description: REST/GraphQL API, web server, or microservice
backend.weight_rationale: Observability is the highest-weighted dimension because backend services fail silently without structured logging and tracing. Safety elevated to 0.10 due to direct exposure to user data and external APIs.

substitute_items.3.4: API response inspection and endpoint health monitoring

backend.notes: Observability and safety are paramount. All Dim 3 items except UI visibility apply fully.

api.name: API Service
api.description: Single REST/GraphQL API service or web server
api.notes: Standard backend — observability and safety focus.

microservice.name: Microservice

substitute_items.3.4: Service mesh visibility and inter-service tracing

microservice.notes: Distributed tracing (3.2) and agent-queryable observability (3.3) are essential.

fullstack.name: Full-Stack Application
fullstack.description: Monolithic full-stack app (frontend + backend in one repo)
fullstack.weight_rationale: Default weights apply as full-stack projects have balanced concerns across all dimensions. Dependency direction between frontend and backend layers is flagged as critical.
fullstack.notes: Default weights apply. Dependency direction between frontend/backend layers is critical.

library.name: Library / Package / CLI Tool
library.description: Reusable library, CLI utility, or developer tool published to npm, PyPI, crates.io, Maven, etc.
library.weight_rationale: Testing and mechanical constraints dominate because libraries are consumed as dependencies — supply chain trust requires robust verification. Safety elevated from 0.02 to 0.05 to reflect supply chain attack vectors (event-stream, ua-parser-js, colors.js incidents). Long-running tasks reduced to 0.02 as libraries rarely involve multi-session agent workflows.

substitute_items.3.2: N/A for libraries and CLI tools
substitute_items.3.3: N/A for libraries and CLI tools
substitute_items.3.4: Can the agent see tool output, exit codes, and error messages clearly?

library.notes: Testing and mechanical constraints are the most important. Observability items for services do not apply.

cli-tool.name: CLI Tool
cli-tool.notes: Focus on diagnostic error context (3.5) in addition to lint and type safety.

client-app.name: Client Application
client-app.description: Desktop or mobile app (Electron, Tauri, React Native, Flutter, SwiftUI, native)
client-app.weight_rationale: Testing elevated to 0.20 because client app regressions are expensive to ship and slow to fix via app store updates. UI visibility is critical for agent-driven development of visual interfaces.

substitute_items.3.2: Application crash reporting and performance monitoring (Firebase, Sentry)
substitute_items.3.3: Device log access and remote debugging capability

client-app.notes: UI visibility and E2E testing are critical. Architecture boundary enforcement matters for multi-process apps.

desktop.name: Desktop Application
desktop.description: Desktop app (Electron, Tauri, Qt, WPF, SwiftUI, native)
desktop.notes: Architecture boundary enforcement matters for multi-process apps.

mobile.name: Mobile Application
mobile.description: Mobile app (React Native, Flutter, SwiftUI, Kotlin/Compose, native iOS/Android)
mobile.notes: Device testing and UI automation are key.

system-infra.name: System / Infrastructure / Specialized
system-infra.description: OS-level code, drivers, embedded systems, game engines, smart contracts, or other safety-critical software
system-infra.weight_rationale: Mechanical constraints and testing are paramount due to safety-critical nature. Safety elevated to 0.10 because errors in system/infra code can cause data loss, security breaches, or hardware damage.

substitute_items.3.4: Hardware-in-the-loop testing, emulator verification, or local blockchain testing

system-infra.notes: Safety rails and rollback are critical. Type safety and mechanical constraints are paramount.

game.name: Game
game.description: Game project (Unity, Unreal, Godot, custom engine)

substitute_items.3.2: In-game debug console and profiler integration

game.notes: Architecture docs crucial (engine vs gameplay separation). Cache-friendly design matters for large asset pipelines.

smart-contract.name: Smart Contract / Blockchain
smart-contract.description: Solidity, Rust (Solana), Move, CosmWasm smart contracts
smart-contract.notes: Immutable deployments make safety and testing the most critical dimensions.

data-ml.name: Data / ML Pipeline
data-ml.description: Data processing, ML training, ETL pipelines, Jupyter notebooks
data-ml.weight_rationale: Long-running tasks elevated to 0.15 because ML training jobs and data pipelines are inherently long-running, requiring durable execution and progress tracking. Testing elevated due to data correctness requirements.
data-ml.notes: Long-running task support is critical (training jobs). Durable execution and progress tracking are essential.

devops-iac.name: DevOps / IaC / Automation
devops-iac.description: Infrastructure as Code, utility scripts, cron jobs, build tools (Terraform, Pulumi, Ansible, Helm)
devops-iac.weight_rationale: Safety is the highest-weighted dimension (0.15) because IaC directly provisions infrastructure — a misconfigured Terraform plan can destroy production resources. Human confirmation gates and rollback capability are critical.

substitute_items.3.4: Terraform plan output review and drift detection

devops-iac.notes: Safety rails are the most critical dimension. Rollback, human confirmation, and least-privilege are paramount.

script-automation.name: Script / Automation
script-automation.description: Utility scripts, cron jobs, one-off automation, build tools
script-automation.notes: Keep it simple. Lint, test, and safety basics. Long-running support matters for scheduled jobs.

monorepo.name: Monorepo
monorepo.description: Multi-package repository (pnpm workspaces, Turborepo, Nx, Lerna, Cargo workspace, Go modules)
monorepo.weight_rationale: Mechanical constraints elevated to 0.22 because cross-package boundary enforcement is essential in multi-package repos. Entropy management elevated to 0.12 due to scale-driven drift.
monorepo.notes: Cross-package boundary enforcement is critical. Entropy management matters more due to scale. See references/monorepo-patterns.md for audit flow.

ai-agent-runtime.name: AI Agent Runtime / Framework
ai-agent-runtime.description: Agent frameworks, LLM orchestrators, autonomous agent runtimes (e.g., OpenClaw, LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen)
ai-agent-runtime.weight_rationale: Context engineering elevated to 0.13 because agent frameworks must manage their own context windows and memory. Long-running tasks elevated to 0.12 due to session persistence requirements. Safety at 0.10 reflects tool protocol trust boundaries.

substitute_items.3.4: Agent session replay and execution trace inspection

ai-agent-runtime.notes: Long-running task support (session persistence, heartbeat, crash recovery) and tool protocol trust (8.6) are defining concerns. Context engineering (Dim 5) covers agent memory management. Agent-queryable observability (3.3) is essential for debugging agent behavior.
